door: noun. A portal of entry into a building, room, or vehicle, typically consisting of a rigid plane movable on a hinge.
dor: noun. Any of species Geotrupes stercorarius of large European dung beetles that make a droning noise while flying.
doth: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of do
hod: verb. To bob up and down on horseback, as an inexperienced rider may do; to jog.
hood: noun. A covering for the head, usually attached to a larger garment such as a jacket or cloak.
hoot: noun. A derisive cry or shout.
hot: adjective. Relating to heat and conditions which produce it.
hotrod: noun. Alternative spelling of hot rod.
odor: noun. Any smell, whether fragrant or offensive.
oho: interjection. Expressing surprise or gloating realisation; aha.
ordo: noun. A musical phrase constructed from one or more statements of one modal pattern and ending in a rest.
ort: noun. A fragment; a scrap of leftover food; any remainder; a piece of refuse.
ortho: noun. An isomer of a benzene derivative having two substituents adjacent on the ring.
orthodox: adjective. Conforming to the accepted, established, or traditional doctrines of a given faith, religion, or ideology.
oxo: noun. A substituent oxygen atom connected to another atom by a double bond.
rho: noun. The seventeenth letter of the Modern Greek and Classical alphabets and the nineteenth letter of Old and Ancient.
rod: noun. A straight, round stick, shaft, bar, cane, or staff.
rood: noun. A crucifix, cross, especially in a church.
root: noun. The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
tor: noun. A craggy outcrop of rock on the summit of a hill, created by the erosion and weathering of rock.
doo: noun. feces, particularly that of a dog.
dort: noun. A sulky or sullen mood; the sulks.
hox: verb. To hock; to hamstring; to cripple; to disable.
oor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oorlams.
oto: noun. An otocinclus (freshwater fish, genus Otocinclus).
rox: verb. Alternative spelling of rocks (“is great”).
tdr: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Todrah.
thd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kuuk Thaayorre.
thoo: preposition. Pronunciation spelling of through, representing African-American Vernacular English.
